𝗦𝗣𝗥𝗜𝗧𝗘𝗙𝗨𝗦𝗜𝗢𝗡-𝗣𝗜𝗫𝗘𝗟-𝗦𝗡𝗔𝗣𝗣𝗘𝗥 | Cleaning and standardizing AI-generated or procedurally created pixel art to fit a strict grid and color palette, making it suitable for game development and scalable assets.  🔥 Super Hot!

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Cleaning and standardizing AI-generated or procedurally created pixel art to fit a strict grid and color palette, making it suitable for game development and scalable assets.
📅 Created: Nov 2025 • Updated: Jun 2026

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Snaps pixels to a perfect grid
✅ Ensures consistent grid resolution
✅ Quantizes colors to a strict palette
✅ Preserves details like dithering
✅ CLI for batch processing and optional arguments (k-colors, pixel-size)
✅ Web (WASM) module for integration into web projects


❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 106 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Sprite Fusion Pixel Snapper is a Rust-based tool designed to clean up messy pixel art, particularly that generated by AI, by snapping pixels to a perfect grid and quantizing colors. It ensures consistency in pixel size and position, making art suitable for game development and scalable textures.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Hugo-Dz/spritefusion-pixel-snapper

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗗𝗡𝗦𝗦𝗘𝗥𝗩𝗘𝗥 | Self-hosting a private and secure DNS server to block ads, malware, and gain network insights.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🔷 C# 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Self-hosting a private and secure DNS server to block ads, malware, and gain network insights.
📅 Created: Oct 2017 • Updated: Jun 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Self-hostable DNS server for privacy and security
✅ Blocks ads and malware at DNS level
✅ Supports DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS, and DNS-over-QUIC
✅ User-friendly web console
✅ Works out-of-the-box with minimal configuration
✅ Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, Linux, macOS, Raspberry Pi)
✅ Docker image available
✅ Built-in Clustering feature
✅ Single Sign-On (SSO) with OpenID Connect (OIDC)
✅ High performance with async IO
✅ Authoritative and recursive DNS server support
✅ DNS logs and stats for network insights


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 8.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 708 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 118 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Technitium DNS Server is an open-source, self-hostable DNS server that enhances privacy and security by blocking ads and malware at the DNS level. It offers advanced features like DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS, and DNS-over-QUIC, and provides network insights through logs and statistics.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/TechnitiumSoftware/DnsServer

𝗙𝗔𝗖𝗘𝗫 | Enabling real-time, on-device face analysis and recognition directly in web applications without server infrastructure.  🌟 Rising Fast!

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 C 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Enabling real-time, on-device face analysis and recognition directly in web applications without server infrastructure.
📅 Created: Apr 2026 • Updated: May 2026

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Runs entirely in the browser via WebAssembly
✅ Includes face detection, 576-point 3D mesh, recognition, and anti-spoofing
✅ Zero server-side dependencies
✅ Trained from scratch with custom models
✅ Encrypted weights (AES-256-GCM) for browser security
✅ Low latency (~3 ms per face)
✅ Part of a larger pure-C stack for embedded systems


▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 262 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 37 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 5 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

FaceX is a comprehensive face processing pipeline that runs entirely within the browser using WebAssembly. It offers face detection, 576-point 3D mesh generation, recognition, and anti-spoofing capabilities without requiring any server-side processing or dependencies.

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/facex-engine/facex

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗧𝗜𝗡𝗨𝗨𝗠 | To enable persistent, collaborative AI coding agent workflows by providing a shared, live codebase context and inter-agent memory.  

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To enable persistent, collaborative AI coding agent workflows by providing a shared, live codebase context and inter-agent memory.
📅 Created: May 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Live code graph with tree-sitter parsing for multiple languages
✅ Hybrid code search combining lexical (BM25) and semantic (model2vec) search
✅ Code navigation features (definitions, callers, dependency graphs)
✅ Cross-agent memory for architectural decisions and action history
✅ Daemon and thin adapter architecture for stateful persistence


✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐ 7 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 0 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Continuum is a persistent, multi-agent Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to provide AI coding agents with a shared, live view of a codebase and a long-term memory. It allows multiple agents to collaborate on a project, maintaining context and state across sessions and agents.

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/redstone-md/Continuum

𝗣𝗢𝗥𝗧𝗔𝗕𝗔𝗦𝗘 | Simplifying and automating the backup and restoration of multiple database types for self-hosted environments.  🔥 Super Hot!

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Simplifying and automating the backup and restoration of multiple database types for self-hosted environments.
📅 Created: Oct 2024 • Updated: Jun 2026

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MsSQL, MariaDB, Firebird SQL, SQLite, MongoDB, Redis, and Valkey
✅ Automated CLI, Docker, Docker Compose, and Kubernetes (Helm) installation
✅ Integrates with Portabase agents for secure operations
✅ Self-hosted and open-source
✅ Provides a dashboard for managing backups


⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 921 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 52 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 17 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Portabase is a self-hosted, open-source tool designed to simplify database backup and restoration across a wide range of popular database systems. It leverages secure agents for operations and offers multiple installation methods including CLI, Docker, and Kubernetes.

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

𝗧𝗘𝗟𝗘𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠-𝗔𝗨𝗧𝗢𝗣𝗜𝗟𝗢𝗧 | Automating responses to Telegram messages using an AI assistant, allowing users to delegate routine communication.  ✨ Notable!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automating responses to Telegram messages using an AI assistant, allowing users to delegate routine communication.
📅 Created: May 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Responds in user's style via system prompt
✅ One-button On/Off control
✅ Two types of notes (contact and AI-generated)
✅ Smart context awareness (sender, history, contact info)
✅ Tone detection and adaptation
✅ Admin dashboard for stats, chat history, prompt editing, model selection
✅ Automatic model fallback
✅ Compatibility with any OpenAI-compatible API


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 15 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 3 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Telegram-autopilot is a personal AI assistant that integrates with your Telegram profile to automatically respond to messages on your behalf. It leverages LLMs and Telegram's official Business Mode to handle routine communication, allowing users to maintain their personal style and context.

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇
